mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when policy is already restrictive and weakens if inflation is still accelerating.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent local low signals labor-market deterioration that eventually pressures earnings expectations and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when unemployment rises only alongside strong output and payroll growth.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points from its 3-month low
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the effect disappears when PAYEMS growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A broad improvement in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which supports near-term equity earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should weaken if retail sales rise while inflation accelerates faster than sales.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with rising RSAFS, or if the 2-year rolling correlation with forward returns is negative while CPIAUCSL is stable.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO with stable labor conditions signals real-side production strength and better near-term corporate output, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails when industrial gains are accompanied by rising unemployment or falling retail sales.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month while UNRATE is flat or falling
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform unconditional 5-day returns, or if positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered observations over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: High FEDFUNDS relative to recent history tightens financing conditions and compresses equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is still elevated, because real rates remain restrictive and the market discounts fewer near-term cuts.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show lower 20-day SPX returns than the sample average, or if the sign turns positive when CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently weak PAYEMS growth signals slowing income formation and weaker consumption, which should lead S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is falsified if payroll weakness is offset by improving unemployment and industrial production.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines in 2 consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: Reject if two-month payroll declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the signal vanishes when UNRATE is falling and INDPRO is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST signals stronger housing demand, construction activity, and wealth effects, which supports risk appetite and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails if housing strength occurs alongside a sharp rise in unemployment.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing increases do not outperform baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect reverses when UNRATE is above its 6-month average.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS both improve, the economy is expanding on both output and employment dimensions, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through stronger growth expectations and cyclical leadership. The mechanism is invalid if either series improves alone without the other.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint improvement condition does not beat unconditional 5-day returns, or if the positive return rate is not at least 5 percentage points higher than when only one of the two series improves.
